title: 2 Setting Up for AFT File Transmission Service
body_text:
2	Setting Up for AFT File Transmission Service
2.1	Overview
This Chapter summarizes the steps and procedures involved in the AFT set-up process. Detailed procedures, including information on mitigating risk, are provided in the Introduction to AFT Part.
The points below summarize the steps the Originator, in conjunction with the financial institution (if applicable), follows to set up for AFT service via File Transmission. The Originator proceeds as follows:
Decides, with the financial institution, which party will input AFT transactions (if the Originator is a business member).
Completes and obtains the appropriate forms and agreements. The documentation required depends on the type of transactions created (PAD or direct deposit) and, if applicable, the type of PADs that will be created (i.e., Cash Management PADs). See Section 2.2, PAD Agreements and Application Forms, and Section 2.3, Direct Deposit Agreements and Application Forms.
Obtains the AFT set up package sent to the financial institution. Business members can arrange for pickup by courier from Central 1 if Central 1 receives authorization from the financial institution. Procedures are emailed directly to the Originator.
Schedules a test run with Central 1. See the Introduction to AFT Part.
2.2	PAD Agreements and Application Forms
The tables below outline the documentation required when generating PAD transactions. Documentation requirements vary according to which party inputs the transactions.
See the Introduction to AFT Part, Chapter 5, Enrolling in the AFT Service, for detailed procedures on completing each of the following forms.
Financial institution inputs transaction for an individual member and uploads files to the File and Report Exchange
Agreement/Form
Comments
Payorâs PAD Agreement, Form 1696
This agreement is made between the Originator (Payee) and the Payor. If the financial institution issues PADs for individual members (e.g., for loan or mortgage payments) it must obtain the agreement from the member (Payor).
Application for AFT Service via FTP, Form 1378
Most financial institutions already have an Originator ID set up for this purpose. A new one is not required each time a member requests a new AFT transaction. Complete only if setting up a new Originator ID.
FTP User Request, Form 2010
Financial institutions may already have an FTP user ID set up for this purpose. A new one is not required each time a member requests a new AFT transaction. Complete only if a financial institution user requires access to the File and Report Exchange for AFT purposes. Refer to the File Exchange and Report Distribution Part for more information on the File and Report Exchange.
Financial institution, on behalf of the business member, inputs transactions and uploads files to the File and Report Exchange
Agreement/Form
Comments
Letter of Undertaking for Pre-authorized Debit Plans, Form 1904
This agreement is between the financial institution and the business member Originator. It is required if the business member Originator will issue Personal PADs or Business PADs.
Letter of Undertaking for Cash Management PADs, Form 2007
This agreement is between the financial institution and the business member Originator. It is required if the business member Originator will issue Cash Management PADs.
Payorâs PAD Agreement, Form 1696
The business member must obtain the agreement from its client (the Payor). It is not required for Cash Management PADs.
Application for AFT Service via FTP, Form 1378
Required for each new Originator
FTP User Request, Form 2010
Financial institutions may already have an FTP user ID set up for this purpose. A new one is not required each time a new Originator ID is added. Complete only if a financial institution user requires access to the File and Report Exchange for AFT purposes. Refer to the File Exchange and Report Distribution Part for more information on the File and Report Exchange.
Related Information
Application for AFT Service via FTP,
Form 1378
Payorâs PAD Agreement,
Form 1696
Letter of Undertaking for Pre-authorized Debit Plans,
Form 1904
Letter of Undertaking for Cash Management PADs,
Form 2007
FTP User Request,
Form 2010
2.3	Direct Deposit Agreements and Application Forms
The tables below outline the documentation required when generating direct deposit transactions. Documentation requirements vary according to which party inputs the transactions.
See the Introduction to AFT Part, Chapter 5, Enrolling in the AFT Service, for detailed procedures on completing each of the following forms.
Financial institution inputs transaction for an individual member and uploads files to the File and Report Exchange
Agreement/Form
Comments
Direct Deposit Application â Business Member Payors, Form 2000
Optional. This form provides the Originator with information on the account to be credited. Alternatively, the customer (Payee) can provide the Originator with a void cheque.
Application for AFT Service via FTP, Form 1378
Most financial institutions already have an Originator ID set up for this purpose. A new one is not required each time a member requests a new AFT transaction. Complete only if a new Originator ID is required.
FTP User Request, Form 2010
Financial institutions may already have a user ID set up for this purpose. A new one is not required each time a member requests a new AFT transaction. Complete only if a financial institution user requires access to the File and Report Exchange for AFT purposes. Refer to the File Exchange and Report Distribution Part for more information on the File and Report Exchange.
Financial institution, on behalf of business member, inputs transactions and uploads files to the File and Report Exchange
Agreement/Form
Comments
Direct Deposit Agreement, Form 2043
This is the agreement between the financial institution and the business member. It is required if the business member Originator will issue Direct Deposits.
Direct Deposit Application â Business Member Payors, Form 2000
Optional. This form provides the Originator with information on the account to be credited. Alternatively, the customer (Payee) can provide the Originator with a void cheque.
Application for AFT Service via FTP, Form 1378
This application is used by all new Originators to apply for AFT service.
FTP User Request, Form 2010
Financial institutions may already have an FTP user ID set up for this purpose. A new one is not required each time a new Originator is added. Complete only if a financial institution user requires access to the File and Report Exchange for AFT purposes. Refer to the File Exchange and Report Distribution Part for more information on the File and Report Exchange.
Related Information
Application for AFT Service via FTP,
Form 1378
Direct Deposit Application â Business Member Payors,
Form 2000
FTP User Request,
Form 2010
Direct Deposit Agreement,
Form 2043
2.4	AFT File Specifications
All new Originators must generate AFT files that adhere to Central 1âs AFT file specifications. These specifications are based on CPA Standard 005, Standards for the Exchange of Financial Data on AFT Files. See Central 1âs AFT File Specifications, Form 2726. Files with an invalid format are rejected at Central 1.
Related Information
Central 1âs AFT File Specifications,
Form 2726
END
